title: Replan proposal store and projection
goal: Introduce the smallest daemon-owned replan proposal lifecycle justified by the RFC: durable proposal records, explicit operator decisions, and status/feed projection of pending decisions without autonomous queue mutation.
acceptance: Daemon state can persist replan proposals with stable ids, source/evidence links, proposed changes, risk, and states pending, accepted, rejected, deferred, and superseded.
Operator decisions are explicit daemon commands or IPC actions that record rationale, authorizer/source, timestamps, and whether a proposal was applied, rejected, deferred, or superseded.

Status JSON and the daemon feed projection render pending/decided proposal state, exact decision commands, and the run's pending-attention reason without adding new RunStatus or SliceStatus enum values.
Interrupted/restarted daemon state preserves pending proposals and does not silently dispatch new workers or repairs against an unknown queue while a required proposal is pending.
No proposal is auto-applied in v1; the auto-approvable tier remains empty unless a later slice changes it with explicit evidence and approval.
Existing runs without proposals preserve current status/watch/monitor behavior except for additive empty proposal/history fields where needed.
must_ask_if: The implementation would introduce an autonomous planner or background replan pass when no finding/proposal exists.
The implementation would auto-apply a queue, slice, verification, or policy change without operator authorization.
The implementation would add a new terminal run status or slice status instead of using projection/pending-attention state.
The implementation would silently edit .workflow/slices during an active run without a durable proposal/revision record.
